svg.text: EXTERNAL INPUTS — from M1 Frozen Declaration Store
⟨ no runtime artifact — purely architectural spec ⟩
• Full generation DAG (all column types unified)
• Topological layer assignment for all columns
• Row generation algorithm — per-row loop + post-generation passes
• target_rows contract received from Phase 1 / M3
rng is a single stateful object passed stage-to-stage; each stage advances its draw sequence
Output: topo_order + rng object (seeded, ready for first draw)
For each non-measure col in topo_order:
• root categorical → sample from weights [rng] • cross-group dep → sample | upstream root [rng]
• child categorical → sample | parent value [rng] • temporal root → sample in [start, end] [rng]
• temporal derived → extract from root (no rng)
Output: rows with all non-measure cols filled
For each measure col in topo_order:
stochastic → _sample_stochastic(col, rows, rng) params = intercept + Σ effects → draw from family(params) [rng]
structural → _eval_structural(col, rows, rng) evaluate formula with upstream values → add noise term [rng]
rows dict → pd.DataFrame (column ordering by topo_order, dtype casting: categorical→object, temporal→datetime64, measure→float64)
Output: df : pd.DataFrame — typed, column-ordered. Subsequent stages operate on DataFrame, not rows dict.
Stage γ — inject_patterns(df, patterns, columns, rng)
Inputs: df (pd.DataFrame), patterns, columns, rng. Current pattern types (outlier_entity, trend_break) are deterministic — rng reserved for future types.
For each pattern: filter rows by target expression (df.eval) → apply pattern transformation (mean-shift / scale factor)
Stage δ — inject_realism(df, realism_config, columns, rng)
⟨ optional: only runs if realism_config is set ⟩
Randomly inject missing (NaN) and dirty (char-perturbed) values [rng selects which cells are affected]
build_schema_metadata(groups, orthogonal_pairs, target_rows, measure_dag_order, columns, group_deps, patterns) → metadata dict (7 keys)
Loop B (max 3 retries): adjusted params + new seed
seed on attempt k = 42 + k → triggers full re-run of §2.8

np.random.default_rng(seed) — NumPy Generator; stateful, deterministic. Same seed → identical output. One object passed α → β → γ → δ; τ_post does not consume rng. Pipeline order: α → β → τ_post → γ → δ → metadata.
